sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.37 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group satisfying the minimum condition for centralizers. Suppose that$X$is a normal subset of$G$such that$[x,y, ,y]=1$for any$x,y X$with$y$repeated$f(x,y)$times. Does$X$generate a locally nilpotent (whence hypercentral) subgroup? If the numbers$f(x,y)$can be bounded, the answer is affirmative (F.\,O.\,Wagner, J.~Algebra, { 217}, no.\,2 (1999), 448--460).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
